p.seg: To all Christ's faithful as well present as future to whom the present writing shall come, Master William of Greenlaw son of Roland sends greeting in the Lord.

I make known to your whole community that moved by divine love and for the salvation of my soul and for the salvation of the souls of all my predecessors and successors I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of Blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God and their Mother and who in perpetuity will serve there three ploughgates of land that I had in the territory of the toun of Hassington with all its pertinents; that is, those that the lady Matilda, mother of the lord Robert de Muschamp, gave to William, son of John of Hownam, with Gilia, daughter of the said Matilda, in free marriage; for the said monks to hold and have with all liberties, easements, and rights belonging to it for ever, in free, pure, and everlasting alms; by performing no secular service whatever for them to anyone, except for prayers and sugffrages and other spiritual goods which the said monks promised in faith, as much as they owe to God and their own Order, that they would perform for me for ever.
And I and my heirs shall maintain to the said monks this alms, and we shall warrant it against all men, and we shall acquit it in respect of every service for ever.
In testimony of this thing my seal has been applied to these presents.
With these witnesses: Ralph of Bunkle; John of Latham; John of Papedy; David of Burradon; Alan of Harcarse; John son of Simon; John of Lambden; Robert his son; Roland son of John; and many others.
